text: Gate aim 'done' on verification evidence appropriate to the aim type
description: For the aim graph to be a trustworthy compass, 'done' must mean verified-done, not claimed-done. Observed repeatedly that aims get closed on weak evidence: a UI-jitter aim closed by git archaeology (visual bug, never seen running), a 'session reflection' aim where the live code path was actually dead. The loop should require + record evidence matched to the aim type before status=done — code: tests/typecheck pass; UI/visual: screenshot or interaction proof; behavior/bugfix: a repro that now passes — captured in the reflection. Complements c0a45822 (grounding VALUE in outcomes) by grounding STATUS in evidence. Without it, more autonomy compounds confident-but-unverified work (drift) rather than capability — the core risk on any path to ASI.
